factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by strong orders and sales growth.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended March 31, 2023, the Company posted record backlog, operating income, operating margins, and operating cash flow, as well as strong orders, sales, net income, and diluted earnings per share.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing significantly, indicating strong demand.

evidence.text: The Company's backlog of unfilled orders at March 31, 2023 was a record $3,433.6 million, an increase of $215.0 million or 6.7% compared with $3,218.6 million at December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2023 compared with the first quarter of 2022

factors.summary: The company is able to mitigate inflationary pressures through pricing actions.

evidence.text: We expect material cost inflation to remain elevated throughout 2023, but anticipate pricing actions to mitigate this inflationary pressure.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher margins, driven by organic growth and acquisitions.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the first quarter of 2023 was due to a 9% increase in organic sales and a 2% increase from acquisitions, partially offset by an unfavorable 2% effect of foreign currency translation.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2023 compared with the first quarter of 2022

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, contributing to overall sales increases.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the first quarter of 2023 was due to a 9% increase in organic sales and a 2% increase from acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the first quarter of 2023 compared with the first quarter of 2022

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflation, but the company is taking actions to mitigate these effects.

evidence.text: During 2022 and into 2023, we continue to experience heightened levels of inflation in material costs, supply chain constraints, as well as continued uncertainty in the global economy. We expect material cost inflation to remain elevated throughout 2023, but anticipate pricing actions to mitigate this inflationary pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to increased sales and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Segment operating margins were 27.7% of net sales for EIG and 25.1% for EMG in the first quarter of 2023, reflecting improvements from the previous year.

factors.summary: The company is taking actions to build inventory and seek alternative sources to mitigate supply chain pressures.

evidence.text: In response to supply chain pressures, we have taken actions to build inventory and seek alternative sources of supply to support sales and backlog growth.

factors.summary: The supply chain is under stress due to constraints, but the company is taking measures to address them.

evidence.text: During 2022 and into 2023, we continue to experience heightened levels of inflation in material costs, supply chain constraints, as well as continued uncertainty in the global economy.

factors.summary: Management expects continued positive impact from backlog, acquisitions, and operational excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: For 2023, our record backlog, the full year impact of the 2022 acquisitions, the 2023 acquisition of Bison Gear & Engineering Corp., and continued focus on and implementation of our Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of our 2023 results.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through growth and expansion of capabilities.

evidence.text: The Company paid $99.3 million, net of cash acquired, to purchase Bison Gear & Engineering Corp. For the first three months of 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving significantly, supporting growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Free cash flow was a record $366.5 million for the first three months of 2023, compared with $175.0 million for the first three months of 2022.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining shareholder returns through dividends and share buybacks.

evidence.text: Effective February 9, 2023, the Company’s Board of Directors approved a 14% increase in the quarterly cash dividend on the Company’s common stock to $0.25 per common share from $0.22 per common share. The Company repurchased $6.5 million of its common stock for the first three months of 2023.
